commentary:
# Cross-Exchange Liquidity Commentary – 2026-07-21

The US equity markets dominate the liquidity hierarchy: NYSE and Nasdaq 100 lead decisively with ILLIQ costs below 0.0057% per $1M traded and comparable volumes exceeding $174.1M, establishing a clear tier-one standard. Tokyo Stock Exchange ranks third despite elevated ILLIQ at 0.0345% per $1M traded, sustained by exceptionally high turnover ratio of 2.9844%, signaling institutional depth despite higher execution costs. Hong Kong Stock Exchange, Euronext, and SIX Swiss Exchange form a mid-tier cluster with moderate ILLIQ ranging from 0.0291% to 0.0444% per $1M traded, though notable divergence appears in SIX's tight 0.5358% AR spread contrasting with its low 0.1610% turnover ratio—suggesting structural tightness masks thin volumes. Korea Exchange and London Stock Exchange occupy the laggard tier, with Korea Exchange displaying acute illiquidity at 0.1404% per $1M traded and punitive 1.4849% AR spread, while LSE's 0.0773% ILLIQ and 0.7875% spread reflect fragmented execution despite European status. Xetra completes the bottom rung with 0.0513% per $1M traded illiquidity and minimal $4.0M comparable volume. For large institutional orders, US venues remain mandatory; for single-name trading in developed Asia-Pacific, Tokyo offers the best depth-adjusted execution despite headline costs.
